food.description: A high-potassium fruit containing ~422mg K+ per medium banana.
food.impact: Dietary K+ overload — cardiac conduction risk
food.mechanism: Your KCNQ1 variant reduces IKs channel expression, impairing the cardiac cell's ability to buffer rapid potassium flux. A single banana delivers 422mg potassium — regular consumption causes cumulative plasma K+ elevation that your kidneys cannot offset quickly enough, prolonging cardiac repolarisation (QT interval). Combined with your PITX2-linked atrial fibrillation risk, potassium-induced QT prolongation creates a compounding arrhythmia trigger, particularly under the current dehydration and travel stress conditions.
risks: QT interval prolongation under dietary K+ load
Atrial conduction disruption — synergistic with PITX2/AFib risk
Hyperkalaemia threshold reached at 2+ bananas per day
Risk amplified by current dehydration from long-haul travel

alternatives.name: Blueberries
alternatives.reason: Only 77mg K+ per cup, safe range

medication.description: Enteric-coated acetylsalicylic acid tablet for pain relief and cardiovascular protection.
medication.mechanism: Your CYP2C9*3 variant (A/C) reduces enzyme activity to ~5% of normal, critically slowing aspirin hydrolysis and salicylate oxidation. At 325mg — four times the low-dose threshold — salicylate plasma levels can accumulate to toxic concentrations. Combined with your cardiovascular gene profile (PITX2, CYP11B2), the risk of aspirin-induced gastric haemorrhage and paradoxical platelet rebound is significantly elevated.
risks: Salicylate plasma accumulation up to 3.8× above expected
Elevated upper GI haemorrhage risk due to COX-1 suppression
Interaction with AFib genetic risk (PITX2) amplifies stroke exposure
